title: Atmospheric pressure air microplasma system for true random bit generation
abstract: There is provided an atmospheric pressure air microplasma system designed for random bit generation including a plurality of plasma electrodes, a power supply module supplying a DC voltage for igniting an arc discharge between the plurality of plasma electrodes, wherein the ignited arc discharge results in establishing and sustaining an arc current channel between the plurality of plasma electrodes, a current probe for measuring and collecting electric current time series data from the arc current channel, and a data acquisition board connected to the current probe for saving the collected electric current time series data, wherein binary sequences are generated from the electric current time series data. Further, the generated binary sequences are proven to pass all 15 tests of NIST Statistical Test Suite and thereby prove to qualify as random sequences.
claims: Claims 1. An atmospheric pressure air microplasma system designed for random bit generation, comprising: - a plurality of plasma electrodes; - a power supply module supplying a voltage for igniting an arc discharge between the plurality of plasma electrodes, wherein the ignited arc discharge results in establishing and sustaining an electric current channel between the plurality of plasma electrodes; - a current probe for measuring and collecting electric current time series data from the electric current channel; and - a data acquisition board connected to the current probe for saving the collected electric current time series data, wherein binary sequences are generated from the electric current time series data. 2. The atmospheric pressure air microplasma system in accordance with claim 1, wherein two plasma electrodes are aligned facing each other at a distance of 1 mm to 1 cm. 3. The atmospheric pressure air microplasma system in accordance with claim 1 or 2, wherein the two plasma electrodes are two needle-like electrodes. 4. The atmospheric pressure air microplasma system in accordance with any one of claims 1 to 3, wherein the binary sequences are generated through a direct decimal-to-binary conversion of the electric current time series data. 5. The atmospheric pressure air microplasma system in accordance with any one of claims 1 to 4, wherein the generated binary sequences are proven to pass all 15 tests of NIST Statistical Test Suite and thereby prove to qualify as random sequences. 6. The atmospheric pressure air microplasma system in accordance with any one of claims 1 to 5, wherein the generated binary sequences qualify as random sequences without requiring post processing of the generated binary sequences. 7. The atmospheric pressure air microplasma system in accordance with any one of claims 1 to 6, wherein the atmospheric pressure air microplasma system is battery-powered thereby resulting in a portable and inexpensive source for true random bit generation (TRBG). 8. The atmospheric pressure air microplasma system in accordance with any one of claims 1 to 7, wherein the power supply module further comprises a MOSFET transistor, a step-up transformer, a diode and an RC filter powered by a rechargeable lithium-ion battery. 9. The atmospheric pressure air microplasma system in accordance with any one of claims 1 to 8, wherein the data acquisition board is further connected to a computer. 10. The atmospheric pressure air microplasma system in accordance with any one of claims 1 to 9, wherein the arc current channel is established between cathodic and anodic tips of the plurality of plasma electrodes, through electron thermionic emission and/or field emission. 11. The atmospheric pressure air microplasma system in accordance with any one of claims 1 to 10, wherein the atmospheric pressure air microplasma system is resilient to external power attacks. 12. The atmospheric pressure air microplasma system in accordance with any one of claims 1 to 11 , wherein the ignited arc discharge is visible or non-visible with the naked eye. 13. A true random bit generator (TRBG) using atmospheric pressure air microplasma as a source of entropy for random bit generation in a cryptographic system, the true random bit generator comprising: - a generator switching circuit for generating a high-voltage microplasma between two electrodes in open air, thereby eliminating a need for optical source and components for random bit generation, wherein the generator switching circuit is powered by a low voltage DC supply. 14. The true random bit generator (TRBG) in accordance with claim 13, wherein the true random bit generator (TRBG) relies on a use of current fluctuations in the atmospheric pressure air microplasma as the source of entropy for random bit generation. 15. The true random bit generator (TRBG) in accordance with claim 13 or 14, wherein electric current time series data measured from the atmospheric pressure air microplasma is used as the sole source of entropy for random bit generation. 16. The true random bit generator (TRBG) in accordance with any one of claims 13 to 15, wherein the true random bit generator (TRBG) eliminates a need for digital post processing of binarized data in order to qualify bit streams as random sequences. 17. The true random bit generator (TRBG) in accordance with any one of claims 13 to 16, wherein the atmospheric pressure air microplasma is generated between tip of a needle electrode and a concentrated anolyte or catholyte of moving interface. 18. The true random bit generator (TRBG) in accordance with any one of claims 13 to 17, wherein the true random bit generator (TRBG) further comprises a passive current sensor interfaced to a computer via a data acquisition module.
